cards.title: Think Your Parent is Neurodivergent? What You Need to Know
cards.body: Up to 97% of autistic people over 60 remain undiagnosed, representing a neglected generation. Experts offer advice for family members on how to support aging parents with undiagnosed neurodiversity.

cards.title: Managing Parental Anxiety During Family Holidays
cards.body: Parental worry can overshadow vacations, but learning to cope with real emergencies builds confidence. One parent discovered that facing her fears head-on helped her become more resilient.

cards.title: Breaking Cycles: Childhood Trauma and Adult Relationships
cards.body: Dysfunctional parenting patterns from childhood can affect adult relationships and romantic partnerships. Learning what healthy relationships look like takes time and conscious effort to break inherited patterns.

cards.title: Only Child Parenting: Challenging Persistent Myths
cards.body: Only children and their parents face outdated stereotypes about being selfish, lonely, or maladjusted. Research shows these prejudices persist despite growing numbers of single-child families.

cards.title: Supporting Your Bright Child's Academic Potential
cards.body: Tutoring isn't the only path to academic success for gifted children. Parents should focus on providing encouragement and support tailored to their child's individual needs.

cards.title: Four Types of Present-but-Absent Fathers
cards.body: Father wounds often stem from emotional unavailability rather than physical absence. Understanding these patterns helps adults address how distant fathers affected their development.

cards.title: Family Happiness Through Mindful Awareness
cards.body: Family life naturally involves change and difficulty, requiring intentional practices to maintain harmony. Mindful awareness helps parents navigate inevitable challenges with greater ease.

cards.title: Preparing Millennials for Equality-Based Parenting
cards.body: Modern couples value shared parenting and equal partnership, but many weren't prepared for this transition. Understanding the gap between expectations and reality helps millennial parents thrive.

cards.title: Smartphones Must Stay Out of English Schools
cards.body: New government guidance requires English schools to be phone-free throughout the entire day. Schools are implementing this policy to reduce distractions and improve student wellbeing.

cards.title: Completing World Cup Sticker Albums on Family Budget
cards.body: Parents use creative strategies like car park meet-ups and Facebook groups to help children complete sticker collections affordably. This hobby has become almost a second job for budget-conscious families.
